title: Send The Right Tipper Tote To Hospital From The VISU_DATA Screen
summary: Use the SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL control on the Operator Station HMI VISU_DATA screen to send the tote currently at the right tipper to the hospital.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when the operator needs to send the tote currently at the right tipper to the hospital using the documented VISU_DATA screen control.
not_for: Do not use this procedure for the left tipper; the source distinguishes SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL from SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL.
Do not use this procedure when confirmation prompts, detailed follow-up indicators, or alternate recovery logic are required; those details are not provided in the source packet.

responsibilities: Locate and press the SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL button.
Verify the action only through source-backed HMI or observable indicators when available.

safety_notes: Use only the documented SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL control on the VISU_DATA screen.
The source packet does not provide confirmation prompt behavior or detailed post-action verification indicators.
If the documented button action does not occur, stop and escalate.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI

access_required: Operator access to the Operator Station HMI
system_preconditions: The Operator Station HMI is available.
The VISU_DATA screen can be opened.
A tote is currently at the right tipper for the documented action to apply.

visual_references.description: Operator Station HMI VISU_DATA screen showing login controls, message banner, and tipper statistics.
Operator station display controls showing how to navigate to the Visu_Data screen.
Additional VISU_DATA screen view showing operator statistics for left and right tippers.

steps.title: Open the VISU_DATA screen
steps.purpose: Access the HMI screen that contains the hospital-routing control for the right tipper.
steps.instruction: Open the VISU_DATA screen on the operator station HMI.
steps.expected_result: The VISU_DATA screen is displayed on the Operator Station HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The VISU_DATA screen is visible and includes the documented data-screen elements such as the informational banner, login/logout area, and tipper-related sections.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot access the VISU_DATA screen or the displayed screen does not match the documented VISU_DATA layout.

actions.label: Open VISU_DATA
actions.exact_action: Open the VISU_DATA screen on the operator station HMI.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: VISU_DATA screen layout with login controls, message banner, and tipper statistics.
Display controls showing navigation to Visu_Data.

stop_or_escalate_if: VISU_DATA cannot be opened from the operator station HMI.
The screen shown does not match the documented VISU_DATA screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The listed control mapping includes F5 to "Visu_Data."

Figure 4-20 and Table 4-6 describe the Operator Station HMI Data Screen, including the informational banner, logged-in user ID, LOGIN/LOGOUT buttons, Tipper Stats, Process Status, RunTime Meters, screen selection drop-down, hospital send buttons, and PAUSE TIPPER.

steps.title: Locate the SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL button
steps.purpose: Identify the specific VISU_DATA control used to route the right-tipper tote to the hospital.
steps.instruction: Locate the SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL button on the VISU_DATA screen.
steps.expected_result: The SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL button is visible on the VISU_DATA screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can clearly identify the SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL control among the VISU_DATA screen controls.
steps.failure_condition: The SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL button is not visible or cannot be distinguished on the VISU_DATA screen.

actions.label: Find hospital-send control
actions.exact_action: Locate the SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL button.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: VISU_DATA screen area containing hospital-routing controls.

stop_or_escalate_if: The SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL button is not present on the VISU_DATA screen.
The operator cannot identify the correct right-side hospital-send control.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 4-20 and Table 4-6 describe the Operator Station HMI Data Screen, including ... SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL, SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL, and PAUSE TIPPER.
Table 4-6 continues with Process Status, RunTime Meters, direct screen selection, SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL, SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL, and PAUSE TIPPER.

steps.title: Confirm the action applies to the right tipper tote
steps.purpose: Ensure the operator is using the documented control for the tote currently at the right tipper.
steps.instruction: Confirm that the action applies to the tote currently at the right tipper.
steps.expected_result: The operator has confirmed that the documented button applies to the tote currently at the right tipper.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote to be routed is the tote currently at the right tipper.
steps.failure_condition: It is unclear whether the tote is at the right tipper, or the operator may be attempting to route the wrong tote.

actions.label: Confirm right-tipper applicability
actions.exact_action: Confirm that the action applies to the tote currently at the right tipper.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: VISU_DATA screen context for the right-side hospital-send control and tipper-related information.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote is not currently at the right tipper.
The operator cannot confirm that the right-side control applies to the intended tote.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL button will send the tote currently at the right tipper to the hospital.

steps.title: Press SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL
steps.purpose: Initiate the documented hospital-send action for the tote currently at the right tipper.
steps.instruction: Press the SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL button on the VISU_DATA screen.
steps.expected_result: The documented hospital-send action is initiated for the tote currently at the right tipper.
steps.healthy_condition: The button press initiates the documented action to send the right-tipper tote to the hospital.
steps.failure_condition: Pressing the button does not initiate the documented hospital-send action.

actions.label: Send right tote to hospital
actions.exact_action: Press the SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL button.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: VISU_DATA screen containing the SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL control.

stop_or_escalate_if: The button does not initiate the documented hospital-send action.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL button will send the tote currently at the right tipper to the hospital.

steps.title: Verify the tote was sent to the hospital
steps.purpose: Confirm the result of the button press using only source-backed visible indicators or direct system observation when available.
steps.instruction: Verify on the HMI or through system observation that the right-tipper tote has been sent to the hospital, if that result is visible from available source-backed indicators.
steps.expected_result: The tote currently at the right tipper has been sent to the hospital.
steps.healthy_condition: Available HMI or observable system behavior is consistent with the tote having been sent to the hospital.
steps.failure_condition: No source-backed indication of successful routing is visible, or observed behavior suggests the tote was not sent to the hospital.

actions.label: Verify hospital routing result
actions.exact_action: Verify on the HMI or through system observation that the right-tipper tote has been sent to the hospital, if that result is visible from available source-backed indicators.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: VISU_DATA screen elements that may reflect the result, if visible.

stop_or_escalate_if: The result cannot be verified from available source-backed indicators.
Observed behavior suggests the tote was not sent to the hospital.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL button will send the tote currently at the right tipper to the hospital.
The VISU_DATA screen includes Tipper Stats, Process Status, RunTime Meters, and an informational banner.

success_criteria: The SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL action is performed from the VISU_DATA screen.
The action applies to the tote currently at the right tipper.
Available source-backed HMI or observable system behavior indicates the tote has been sent to the hospital.
healthy_conditions: The VISU_DATA screen is accessible on the Operator Station HMI.
The SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL button is present and identifiable.
The tote to be routed is currently at the right tipper.
Pressing the button initiates the documented hospital-send action.
failure_conditions: The VISU_DATA screen cannot be opened.
The SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL button cannot be found on the VISU_DATA screen.
The tote is not confirmed to be at the right tipper.
The button does not initiate the documented hospital-send action.
The source packet does not provide detailed confirmation prompts or explicit follow-up indicators.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the button does not initiate the documented hospital-send action.
Escalate if the operator cannot verify the result using available source-backed indicators.
Escalate if the correct VISU_DATA control cannot be located or the screen does not match the documented layout.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: VISU_DATA screen showing the documented layout and hospital-routing controls.
Navigation controls used to access Visu_Data.
Additional VISU_DATA screen context for left/right tipper information.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL button will send the tote currently at the right tipper to the hospital.

Figure 4-20 and Table 4-6 describe the Operator Station HMI Data Screen, including the informational banner, logged-in user ID, LOGIN/LOGOUT buttons, Tipper Stats, Process Status, RunTime Meters, screen selection drop-down, hospital send buttons, and PAUSE TIPPER.
The listed control mapping includes F5 to "Visu_Data."

missing_details: The source does not provide confirmation prompt behavior for pressing SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL.
The source does not provide explicit post-action indicators that confirm successful hospital routing.
The source does not specify whether login is required before using the SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL button.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not state whether production stop or LOTO is required.
